items.title: Gaza: Israeli strike wipes out doctor's family, says Hamas | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A tragic story has emerged from southern Gaza. The Hamas-run civil defense agency says an Israeli air strike in the city of Khan Yunis has hit the home of a doctor, wiping out most of her family. Israel says it was targeting Hamas operatives in the area. On Monday, the military issued forced evacuation orders for Gaza’s second-largest city, warning of an "unprecedented attack". 

Chapters:
0:00 The latest from Gaza
3:42 Balig Sladeen, Journalist

items.title: How credible are Trump's new tariff threats? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The European Union has responded to Donald Trump's newest threats on trade. EU Trade Commissioner Marosh Sefcovic said mutual respect is needed, and not tariffs. The US president had re-ignited global tensions - by threatening to impose a 50 percent tariff on imports from the European Union.

items.title: Russia launches missile and drone attack on Kyiv | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russia and Ukraine have exchanged more prisoners on the second day of a major swap. Each side handed over 307 soldiers, a day after exchanging another 390 each. Meanwhile, at least 15 people have been injured in a large-scale Russian drone and missile attack focused on the Ukrainian capital Kyiv. With ceasefire efforts otherwise stalled, Ukraine's President Voldymyr Zelenskyy said new international sanctions are needed to force Russia to agree to a truce. 

00:00 Russia launches missile and drone attack on Kyiv
02:27 Rebecca Ritters, DW Correspondent

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In Georgia, fear of Russia is growing. Many Georgians are scared that, if Ukraine falls, their country could be Putin’s next target. Until now, they have been protected by the US. But their trust has been shaken.

items.title: Nigeria receives largest single return of Benin Bronzes artifacts | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A Dutch museum is taking a historic step... sending more than a hundred precious artefacts back to Nigeria. The Wereldmuseum in Leiden is preparing a collection of Benin Bronzes looted during a violent British raid in 1897.This marks the largest single return of these treasures to date.

items.title: Undersea cables: A hidden war beneath the waves? | Mapped Out
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Strange things are happening in the Baltic Sea. It’s a hotspot for undersea communications cables. And increasingly an area where geopolitical tensions between NATO states and Russia are on the rise. A series of incident in which undersea infrastructure was damaged led NATO countries to suspect systematic sabotage. We'll explore what evidence there really is, how the incidents fit into the wider context of Russia's hybrid war and evaluate how NATO is reacting.

00:00 Strange incidents in the Baltic Sea   
03:28 Accidents or sabotage?  
07:12 Russia's possible means and motives 
11:25 NATO’s dilemma: How to react 

Watch our episode on Kaliningrad here: https://youtu.be/44Fot5C4hs4?si=0hnBptw-RyzA-ulN 
Watch our episode on Kaliningrad on Taiwan here: https://youtu.be/kMhle4o0uk0?si=8L6Uf0o9BoN9hAbY

items.title: What consequences a new EU-Trump tariff war could have | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump has reignited global trade tensions – by threatening to impose a 50 per cent tariff on imports from the European Union. 

The US president accuses EU member states of using unfair trade practices. In his view, they include value added taxes and unjustified lawsuits against American companies. Trump adds that the EU is running a huge trade surplus with America – and that negotiations to reduce this are going nowhere. 

US and European stocks have tumbled in response to his remarks. In a statement from the Oval Office, Trump renewed his pressure on European companies to move production to the US. 

00:00 Looming EU-US trade war?
01:08 George Magnus, Economist

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Why Iran's nuclear program is so important for the country | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Delegations from Iran and the US have finished a fifth round of nuclear talks in Rome – mediated by Oman. 

The Omani foreign minister says the two sides made some progress. He said the proposals will be reviewed by both governments, and another round will be scheduled accordingly. US President Donald Trump wants to limit Tehran's potential to produce nuclear weapons and Iran is hoping to have sanctions lifted. Trump has threatened military strikes on Iran if a deal is not reached. 

00:00 Intro
01:03 Cornelius Adebahr, German Council on Foreign Relations 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: India-Pakistan clash: the dangerous ‘new normal’ | Global Eyes
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Both India and Pakistan are claiming victory in their most recent violent clash over Kashmir. Indian PM Narendra Modi and his counterpart in Islamabad Shehbaz Sharif however welcomed the truce, which US President Donald Trump proudly claimed had ended the 500 years of fighting between the two sides —Kashmir has repeatedly been a source of conflict between India and Pakistan since the end of British colonial rule in 1947.
China’s close relationship with Pakistan appears to have become even closer. Pakistan Army Chief—Field Marshal Aasim Munir has seen his public approval rating soar. Islamabad relied on Chinese-made missiles and aircraft which faced India’s Western-made military technology for the first time.  Both sides also deployed drones in the conflict. India has said Pakistan will not get water from Indian-controlled rivers—which would breach water sharing agreements that have withstood previous wars between the two nations.
And what of the people of Kashmir—what do they want for their future and is anybody asking them?
Guests Praveem Donthi of the International Crisis Group and indpendent analyst Sahar Khan join our hosts to look at the domestic, regional and interntional implications of the clash between the nuclear-armed rivals. Are there really any winners? 

भारत-पाकिस्तान संघर्ष: खतरनाक है ये 'नई हकीकत'

 
भारत और पाकिस्तान के बीच हालिया हिंसक टकराव के बाद दोनों देश खुद को विजेता बता रहे हैं. हालांकि, भारतीय प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी और पाकिस्तान के प्रधानमंत्री शहबाज शरीफ ने उस संघर्षविराम का स्वागत किया है, जिसे अमेरिकी राष्ट्रपति डॉनल्ड ट्रंप ने गर्व से "पांच सौ साल पुराने संघर्ष का अंत" बताया.
 
1947 में ब्रिटेन से आजादी मिलने के बाद से भारत और पाकिस्तान के बीच कश्मीर बार-बार संघर्ष का कारण रहा है. इस बार चीन और पाकिस्तान के रिश्ते और भी गहरे होते दिखे. पाकिस्तान के सेना प्रमुख फील्ड मार्शल आसिम मुनीर की लोकप्रियता में जबरदस्त बढ़ोतरी हुई है. इस संघर्ष में इस्लामाबाद ने चीन निर्मित मिसाइलों और लड़ाकू विमानों का इस्तेमाल किया, जो पहली बार भारत की पश्चिमी तकनीक से लैस सैन्य ताकत से टकराए.
 
दोनों देशों ने इस संघर्ष में ड्रोन तकनीक का भी इस्तेमाल किया. भारत ने यह भी कहा है कि वह अपने यहां से होकर बहने वाली नदियों से पाकिस्तान को पानी नहीं देगा - जो कि दोनों देशों के बीच लंबे समय से चले आ रहे जल समझौतों का उल्लंघन होगा. ये समझौते अब तक कई युद्धों के बावजूद कायम रहे हैं.
 
लेकिन इस सबके बीच कश्मीर के लोगों की बात कौन कर रहा है? वे अपने भविष्य को लेकर क्या चाहते हैं? क्या कोई उनसे पूछ भी रहा है?
 
इस विषय पर चर्चा के लिए इंटरनेशनल क्राइसिस ग्रुप के प्रवीन डोंठी और स्वतंत्र विश्लेषक सहर खान हमारे मेजबानों के साथ जुड़ते हैं. वे इस टकराव के घरेलू, क्षेत्रीय और अंतरराष्ट्रीय असर का विश्लेषण करते हैं. सवाल यह भी है - क्या इस संघर्ष में वाकई कोई विजेता है?
 

 اکستان اور بھارت کے درمیان جھڑپیں: خطرناک ’نیو نارمل‘

 
کشمیر تنازعہ: نتائج کا جائزہ   
بھارتی زیر انتظام کشمیر میں ہونے والے ایک حملے کے بعد بھارت اور پاکستان کے درمیان ہونے والی جھڑپوں میں دونوں ممالک ہی اپنی کامیابی کا دعویٰ کر رہے ہیں۔ بھارتی وزیر اعظم نریندر مودی اور اسلام آباد میں ان کے ہم منصب شہباز شریف نے تاہم اس فائر بندی کا خیر مقدم کیا، جس کے بارے میں امریکی صدر ڈونلڈ ٹرمپ نے فخریہ طور پر دعویٰ کیا تھا کہ اس سے دونوں فریقوں کے درمیان 500 سال سے جاری لڑائی کا خاتمہ ہوا۔ 1947 میں برطانوی نوآبادیاتی حکمرانی کے اختتام کے بعد سے کشمیر مسلسل بھارت اور پاکستان کے درمیان وجہ تنازعہ بنا ہوا ہے۔ پاکستان کے ساتھ چین کے تعلقات مزید قریبی ہوتے دکھائی دے رہے ہیں۔ پاکستان کے آرمی چیف فیلڈ مارشل عاصم منیر کی مقبولیت میں اضافہ دیکھنے میں آیا ہے۔ اسلام آباد نے چینی ساختہ میزائلوں اور طیاروں پر انحصار کیا جو پہلی مرتبہ بھارت کی مغربی ساختہ ملٹری ٹیکنالوجی کے مدمقابل آئے۔  دونوں فریقوں نے اس تنازعے میں ڈرون بھی استعمال کیے۔ بھارت کا کہنا ہے کہ پاکستان کو اس کی جانب سے آنے والے دریاؤں سے پانی نہیں ملے گا، جو پانی کی تقسیم کے معاہدوں کی خلاف ورزی ہے، حالانکہ یہ معاہدہ اس سے قبل ہونے والی جنگوں میں بھی قائم رہا تھا۔ اور پھر کشمیر کے عوام کا کیا ہوگا؟ وہ اپنے مستقبل کے لیے کیا چاہتے ہیں اور کیا کوئی ان سے بھی اس بارے میں پوچھ رہا ہے؟  ہمارے مہمان پراویم ڈونتھی، جو بین الاقوامی کرائسز گروپ سے منسلک ہیں اور معروف تجزیہ کار سحر خان، ہمارے میزبانوں کے ساتھ مل کر ایٹمی ہتھیار رکھنے والے ان دو حریف ملکوں کے درمیان تصادم کے داخلی، علاقائی اور بین الاقوامی مضمرات کا جائزہ لیں گے۔ کیا واقعی کوئی فاتح ہے؟ 
پاکستان اور بھارت کے درمیان جھڑپیں: خطرناک ’نیو نارمل‘

items.title: "He likes to cleanse Gaza of its Palestinians" What's behind Netanyahu's 'sterile zones'? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has been criticized by allies over the worsening humanitarian crisis. But he says he has a three-part plan: The first phase is approving basic food deliveries. The second would see US companies distribute food. In the third phase, a so-called 'sterile zone' free of Hamas would be created - where Palestinians should move to receive aid. Netanyahu also said that Palestinians who go to the planned 'sterile zone' in the south "don't necessarily go back."

0:00 Israel plans to establish 'sterile zones' for Palestinians in Gaza
0:57 Mairav Zonszein, Senior Analyst on Israel-Palestine for the International Crisis Group

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Could tariffs really move production back to the US? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Wall Street shares have been tumbling after US President Donald Trump reignited trade tensions, threatening a 50% tariff on the EU and at least 25% on Apple if it does not shift iPhone production to the US. The move has shifted market focus back to Trump's aggressive trade stance after the Republican-controlled US House of Representatives passed the president's sweeping tax and spending bill.

00:00 Intro
00:57 Abigail Hall, Associate Professor, University of Tampa

#Trump #Tariffs #Economy
